definition: Quintessence is the 'fifth essence' — Aristotle's incorruptible celestial element beyond earth, water, air, and fire — and, to the alchemists, the refined spirit distilled out of gross matter.
history: From Medieval Latin *quinta essentia*, translating Aristotle's aether. John of Rupescissa, a 14th-century Franciscan, distilled alcohol and called it the quintessence, a heavenly incorruptible drawn from the earthly to fortify the body against decay. The word now means simply the purest example of a thing.

def_sources.label: Lyndy Abraham, A Dictionary of Alchemical Imagery
Leah DeVun, Prophecy, Alchemy, and the End of Time

etymology.origin: Medieval Latin quinta essentia ‘the fifth essence’
etymology.note: Aristotle’s incorruptible fifth element (after earth, water, air, fire); the alchemists sought to distil it out of matter.
